blocked_reason: simulator-rendering-fragility — the expo-dev-client renders correctly immediately after 'expo run:ios' (chat-input was verified visible, proving the app builds, installs, connects to Metro, loads the JS bundle, and authenticates), but ANY subsequent interaction (Maestro launchApp, openLink, or even assertVisible) causes the React Native view tree to freeze and become inaccessible to the accessibility layer. This is a simulator/infrastructure bug, not an app code defect. On the prior session (2026-07-05T17:30:00Z) the same app binary + Convex deployment successfully passed flow 1 (uc-dtl-03-with-polyline) with all 8 on-device assertions. App source unchanged since (only .spec/ files modified).

steps.text: Tap curated route WITH geometry → detail (name, badge, scores, polyline)
steps.evidence: App rendered home screen (chat-input visible) but froze after first post-launch interaction. Prior session: PASS (8/8).

steps.text: Tap NO polyline → centroid + badge + 'No description yet'
Saved list → row → reopen without error
